sections.paragraph:
σφυρόν, οῦ, τό

① ankle (Hom. et al.; EpArist 87; Jos., Ant. 3, 155; 7, 171).

② heel (Eur., Alc. 586; Ps.-Oppian, Cyn. 3, 143); both are poss. in Ac 3:7 v.l. S. σφυδρόν.—DELG.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Ps.-Oppian Ps.-Oppian = Oppian of Apamea, imitator of Oppian of Anazorbos in Cilicia, II–III a.d.—List 5

poss. poss. = possible

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 981
